title: radermachera ghorta
intro: Radermachera ghorta, also known as the Mallard Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Radermachera ghorta include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and treat respiratory infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves in water, and it can also be used as a topical treatment by applying a paste made from its roots to the affected area.
